Template Use Case/Title: green location NA Specific Pushback on FedEx Daily Pickups (SP)
Text: Hi [SUPPLIER NAME], Thank you for reaching out. I'm happy to address this for you today. If you do not currently have daily pickups from Fedex, you can schedule your own pickups by calling Fedex when needed as a "Next Day On Call" (NDOC). Through this process, you are responsible for calling FedEx to request a next day pickup. You may find the procedure below. Please call FedEx (1-800-GO-FEDEX; say "Ground Pickup"; provide your own FedEx Account Number and Address) by 4PM to request a Next Day Pickup. You must use your own FedEx account number to schedule this pickup. You can also register for pickup online (Schedule a one-time pickup or recurring pickups | FedEx). Scheduling a "one time pickup" is the online equivalent of NDOC. You will receive a pickup the next day in the time window committed by FedEx. Product must be ready for pickup by the beginning of the pickup window. FedEx will induct the product into their network that evening, triggering a Ship Confirm notification to the customer If you use Partner Home to print FedEx labels, it is absolutely imperative that you schedule the NDOC pickup using your FedEx account number (associated with your physical shipping address). If the supplier does not use the correct address, they are responsible for any failures to receive pickups. This process enables all packages to be shipped within 24 hours. You can also contact your local FedEx sales representative to enroll in this process. As outlined in our contract, Wayfair is not responsible for and will not reimburse pick up fees (charged by FedEx directly to the supplier). It is true that there is an additional charge associated with NDOC, but the weekly maximum fee for NDOC of $20 is $3/week more expensive than recurring ground pick ups ($17/week). Please let us know if you have questions. Thanks again for reaching out. I am marking this ticket as complete. If you need further support with this issue, please comment to re-open. Help us improve by sharing your feedback. You will receive an email shortly with a survey, or you can provide feedback on any closed tickets in Partner Home. We sincerely value your partnership. Thank you,

Applicable SOPs: NMFC and Freight Code Requests SOP (DSC2S)
Text: Hi {SU name}/Team, Thank you for reaching out. To ensure your products are classified correctly, we recommend referencing the National Motor Freight Traffic Association (NMFTA) guidelines. Because freight classes and NMFC codes are highly specific to individual items factoring in density, handling, stowability, materials used, intended use of items and liability, referring directly to these guidelines is the best way to ensure the accuracy of your specific catalog.
